meaning: adj.鍍忔按姣嶇殑,姘存瘝鐘剁殑
examples: No alternation of generations the medusoid phase being entirely suppressed: sea anemones; corals.
Coelenterates typically having alternation of generations; hydroid phase is usually colonial giving rise to the medusoid phase by budding: hydras and jellyfishes.
When set free in a container of seawater, the muscles could be jolted into action with a small electric charge to produce synchronised contractions, allowing Medusoid to swim.
The current version of Medusoid moves in a simple manner and can't really turn or maneuver.
The robot, named "Medusoid, " was placed in salty fluid that can conduct electrical currents.
